styleHead: STYLE LOCK: Period gothic live-action, mid-19th-century interiors; candlelight and deep shadows; desaturated cool-gray palette; naturalistic skin and fabric texture; no illustration or stylized animation
castCountLine: CAST COUNT: exactly 1 distinct on-screen character identity(ies) only — Character_The_Old_Man. Do not invent extra people, duplicate faces, or crowd extras not listed.
actionText: INT. OLD MAN'S BEDCHAMBER - NIGHT. The Old Man A single dim ray, like the thread of a spider, shoots from the lantern and falls full upon the vulture eye. It is open-wide, wide open. OFF-CAMERA VOICEOVER Character_The_Narrator says "I saw it with perfect distinctness - all a dull blue, with a hideous veil over" Camera directive: Macro hold on the hideous veiled eye, ray steady, texture of the dull blue filling the frame completely. Performance: Acting intensity 7/10: Unblinking hyper-focused stare, brows sharp, lip twitch of pure revulsion Optics: f/1.4 shallow depth of field, creamy background bokeh Color grading: Kodak Vision3 500T 5219, crushed charcoal shadows, cold blue-black tones, single sickly amber lantern ray Character_The_Old_Man is on screen.
prompt:
STYLE LOCK: Period gothic live-action, mid-19th-century interiors; candlelight and deep shadows; desaturated cool-gray palette; naturalistic skin and fabric texture; no illustration or stylized animation

CHARACTER VARIABLES (use these identities consistently; do not redesign faces or wardrobe):
- Character_The_Narrator [The Narrator]: Also present (not shot focus); keep identity consistent: Lean man of middle years (about 40–50), pale sallow skin,....
- Character_The_Old_Man [The Old Man]: Frail elderly man, thin stooped build, sparse white hair, deeply lined pale face; one pale blue eye with a dull filmy veil, the other eye ordinary; wears a plain white period nightshirt. Visual lock: Always elderly, white-haired, frail; signature constant is the single pale blue eye with dull filmy veil that must not drift to clear blue or to the Narrator's face; nightshirt is his default on-screen garment. Voice: Elderly male; thin, reedy, low energy; short startled phrases; weak breath support; same fragile voice in the single spoken line and any murmur.

CAST COUNT: exactly 1 distinct on-screen character identity(ies) only — Character_The_Old_Man. Do not invent extra people, duplicate faces, or crowd extras not listed.

AUDIO: REQUIRED native Grok off-camera voiceover. Character_The_Narrator narrates exactly: "I saw it with perfect distinctness - all a dull blue, with a hideous veil over". Start speaking immediately with "I" — do not skip, delay, or swallow the opening word. After the last word, hold a brief natural pause with a closed mouth (about half a second); do not freeze mid-syllable or trail into empty staring. Do not lip-sync on-screen cast to this VO. Secondary layer = soft room tone / Foley. VOICE LOCK Character_The_Narrator: Male, middle years; medium-high tense pitch; precise, controlled pace that sharpens into fevered urgency; intimate confessional energy, same voice on-camera and in V.O.

PREVIOUS CLIP (already provided as video input — continue from its last frame):
INT. OLD MAN'S BEDCHAMBER - NIGHT. The Old Man A single dim ray, like the thread of a spider, shoots from the lantern and falls full upon the vulture eye. It is open-wide, wide open. OFF-CAMERA VOICEOVER Character_The_Narrator says "It was open — wide, wide open — and I grew furious as I gazed upon it." Camera directive: Extreme close-up on the open vulture eye, single spider-thread lantern ray slicing across the dull blue iris. Performance: Acting intensity 7/10: Widening furious eyes locked on the eye, nostrils flaring, jaw clenched Optics: f/1.4 shallow depth of field, creamy background bokeh Color grading: Kodak Vision3 500T 5219, crushed charcoal shadows, cold blue-black tones, single sickly amber lantern ray Character_The_Old_Man is on screen.
